meaning: n.<主英>气味,香味,臭味,意味,名声
examples: Elaborate precautions were taken to prevent the foraging team using odour clues.
"It smells like…," we have to say when describing an odour, struggling to express our olfactory experience.
He was acutely aware of the odour of cooking oil.
Clear water with no odour and a clean and crisp taste earns higher marks.
When my nose first got a whiff of its pungent, acidic odour, my stomach began to churn.
